nasb.text: They are like children who sit in the market place and call to one another, and they say, ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not weep.’

esv.text: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling to one another, “‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not weep.’

cnv.text: 他們好像小孩子坐在巿中心，彼此呼叫，說： ‘我們給你們吹笛子，你們卻不跳舞； 我們唱哀歌，你們也不啼哭。’

ccb.text: 他们就如街头上戏耍的孩童——彼此呼叫， “‘我们吹娶亲的乐曲， 你们不跳舞； 我们唱送葬的哀歌， 你们不哭泣。’

csbs.text: 他们就好比：孩子们坐在街市上，彼此呼叫，说： ‘我们为你们吹笛， 你们却不跳舞； 我们唱哀歌， 你们也不哭泣。’
